title: list-tags ignores mirrors?
body:
Running `skopeo list-tags docker://debian`, v1.1.1 on Ubuntu 18.04

`/etc/containers/registries.conf`:

```toml
unqualified-search-registries = ['mirror1.redacted', 'mirror2.redacted']

[[registry]]
prefix = "docker.io"
# location =  "registry-1.docker.io"
location = "mirror1.redacted"

[[registry.mirror]]
location = "mirror1.redacted"

[[registry.mirror]]
location = "mirror2.redacted"
```

I have set a similar configuration in `/etc/docker/daemon.json`.

(both mirrors are running `registry:2` set in proxy/mirror mode)

If I override `registry-1.docker.io` in `/etc/hosts`, I get this:

```
FATA[0060] Error listing repository tags: error pinging docker registry registry-1.docker.io: Get https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/: dial tcp 192.0.2.0:443: i/o timeout
```

This would strongly indicate that `list-tags` is ignoring my registry settings?

I'm getting the same error for `docker://library/debian` and `docker://docker.io/library/debian`

comments.body: That's correct. Mirrors are only used when pulling an image (i.e., on the source of a copy operation).
That seems like a bug? Or at least could lead to unexpected results in some configurations.

The way I’m thinking about mirrors (which is may not be the way anyone else thinks, and isn’t documented anywhere), they are not _intentionally specifically_ restricted to pulls (e.g. pushes are not included because it’s much harder / impossible to do across unsynchronized destinations, not because it’s fundamentally not interesting to do).

It might well make sense to implemented mirrors for list-tags. OTOH…
> Or at least could lead to unexpected results in some configurations.
this suggests the situation where mirrors only have a subset of images available.

For pulls, that’s an _expected and valuable benefit_ — but that’s also a good reason _not to_ use mirrors for listing tags.

Also, I’d argue that a `skopeo sync` by the mirror admin is one of the cases where the user is _clearly aware_ of the mirroring topology, so the user can easily change the `skopeo sync` command line to read directly from the intended mirror instead of referencing the primary registry and expecting the mirror configuration to apply.

So, on balance I’m leaning towards not changing the current implementation; but there may well be something I’m missing.

---

I do agree that the documentation should be clarified.

Don't forget that there's also dynamic transparent mirrors, such as `registry:2` in mirror mode.

In theory, you could also have a mirror that applies some filtering (eg, to implement corporate policy) or is a mix of pull-thru and local repositories. In both these cases, the different sources of data will lead to bugs and inconsistent data. (Granted, to my knowledge no software has been published that implements this. But you could also just hack it together with squid or other httpd.)
I think we want to pass on this.  Closing.

Granted this issue was closed prior to Docker imposing pull rate limits based on the number of manifest requests, but only respecting the registry mirror configuration on pulls is especially frustrating when the upstream registry is not available (e.g., rate limited, airgapped environments). Am I correct in my understanding that the options are:

- Figure out how to accomplish non-pull operations through mirrors some other way; or, 
- Always pull the image, because what's 500MB of bandwidth between friends?

Instead of restricting skopeo's functionality, what about providing some command line options to ignore (or force the use of) mirrors for those of us who need to live dangerously?

@AstraLuma Were you able to workaround your issue with `list-tags` using some other magic?

Not really. Skopeo isn't in common use in my environment, and the company later made additional arrangements with Docker Hub.

I will note that I discovered this issue as part of prep work for said rate limiting.

> Granted this issue was closed prior to Docker imposing pull rate limits based on the number of manifest requests, but only respecting the registry mirror configuration on pulls is especially frustrating when the upstream registry is not available (e.g., rate limited, airgapped environments). Am I correct in my understanding that the options are:
> 
> * Figure out how to accomplish non-pull operations through mirrors some other way; or,
> * Always pull the image, because what's 500MB of bandwidth between friends?

Could you start by explaining what _exactly_ is the whole situation / use case, please?

For `skopeo sync` I think the callers should usually be aware of mirrors. Is that incorrect? Similarly `skopeo list-tags` feels like a _somewhat_ unusual operation (not truly _rare_, just not something that I’d expect to happen all the time during ordinary application deployment), likely to be used in administrative scripts that need to be aware already.

It’s quite possible, or perhaps even likely, the above data-less theory is incorrect, but I don’t know how.

`list-tags` is not something that happens during deployment flows.

But it can happen as part of auditing or automation workflows (eg, automatically create timestamped tags or mirroring to a private repo for retention purposes. I might use it to automatically create overlaid images that adds $DAYJOB's private certificates).

There's honestly just not many ways to inspect a remote repo without downloading the data.
